### New Lead Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Score). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Download Source File

### Download Source File

GET ={{ $json.fileUrl }} — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Parse CSV Contents

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming lead records to a stable schema

Next: Load Rows into PostgreSQL

### Load Rows into PostgreSQL

Runs a parameterized `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT` upsert — parameters are bound via `queryReplacement`, never string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Next: Align Field Names

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Send Summary to Discord

### Send Summary to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

# Capture form submissions with PostgreSQL and Discord without manual work

**Pattern:** Form Intake · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Capture form submissions with PostgreSQL and Discord without manual work. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual enrichment is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Lead Form Submission → Download Source File → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Load Rows into PostgreSQL → Align Field Names → Tag Pipeline Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to form intake without manual effort
- Anyone automating enrichment in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Discord webhook URL; Postgres connection.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your enrichment process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
